texture_channel_convention.ALB: sRGB base/albedo with baked procedural color variation
texture_channel_convention.RMA: linear R=metallic intent, G=roughness intent, B=ambient occlusion/grime intent; Standard material scalar smoothness remains authoritative until shader import is tuned
texture_channel_convention.GRM: linear R=edgewear/chip mask, G=grime/soot deposit mask, B=wetness/patina mask

materials.use: Corridor wall blocks, low wall returns, wet floor edges
materials.description: Oil-dark stone slabs with recessed mortar, cracks, pooled wetness, and amber glints.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate. This directly attacks the flat grey wall/floor gap.

materials.use: Dense wall/floor tiling where visible mortar detail is needed
materials.description: Finer black-stone blockwork with damp mortar lines and grime held in cracks.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate, but should be scale-tested in Unity before broad tiling.

materials.use: Primary corridor wall plates and doors
materials.description: Riveted dark iron panels with chipped brass/steel edges, oxidized seams, and oil smears.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate. This is the set anchor for black riveted iron.

materials.use: Secondary plates, weapon receiver blocks, service door panels
materials.description: Darker service plating with tighter rivet rhythm, scraped corners, and soot-set seams.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate for dark panels; slightly busy at distance by design.

materials.use: Wall/ceiling pipes, weapon barrels, valve housings
materials.description: Aged brass cylinder surface with lengthwise scratches, rubbed highlights, tarnish, and banding.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate. Replaces clean yellow brass with worn pressure hardware.

materials.use: Valve wheels, receivers, fittings, gauge bezels
materials.description: Cast brass with darker patina pockets, raised rim wear, stamped band rhythm, and soot dust.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate for props; not a wall/floor hero.

materials.use: Steam coils, heat exchangers, pressure weapon tubing
materials.description: Copper with green-blue oxidation in grooves and fresh copper rubs on exposed coil ridges.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate for breaking up brass monotony.

materials.use: Old pipe backs, leaky walls, drainage-adjacent metal
materials.description: Dirtier copper sheet with vertical verdigris runoff and darker soot deposits.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate, best used sparingly because the green can become loud.

materials.use: Pressure warnings, gate strips, hazard caps
materials.description: Deep red enamel over metal with chips, scratches, black soot specks, and warm worn edges.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate for pressure danger language.

materials.use: Weapon details, panel edges, valve warning rims
materials.description: Heavier chipped red enamel exposing dark iron and brass undertones around edges and seams.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate but high-detail; should be kept to accents.

materials.use: Lantern glass, status windows, small warm highlights
materials.description: Warm amber glass with smoke scratches, grime, and bright gaslight lens bloom baked into base color.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate for atmosphere. Needs actual light/VFX later for full glow.

materials.use: Gauge covers and small instrument windows
materials.description: Smokier amber glass with fingerprints, grime arcs, and heavier dark edges for instrument covers.
materials.blunt_note: Candidate. Reads well in previews, but transparency needs a project shader decision.

materials.use: Future decal/overlay use near vents, weapons, furnace mouths
materials.description: Matte black-brown soot clouds with ash speckles and directional exhaust deposits.
materials.blunt_note: Candidate overlay. It is authored as an opaque material plus masks until a decal shader is chosen.

materials.use: Future decal/overlay use under pipes, corners, and wall seams
materials.description: Long oily vertical streaks with wet centers and dusty feathered sides.
materials.blunt_note: Candidate overlay. Needs alpha/decal hookup before production placement.

materials.use: Foundry floors, puddle-heavy service walkways
materials.description: Black stone/iron floor with oily puddles, scuffed traffic lanes, and amber wet reflections.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate. Use in patches so the level does not become uniformly glossy.

materials.use: Local puddle surfaces, drainage zones, floor decals later
materials.description: Very wet black puddle sheet with rainbow-dark oil edges and soft amber highlights.
materials.blunt_note: Candidate. Final look is strong; gameplay readability must be checked because it is very dark.

materials.use: Furnace doors, heated boiler plates, muzzle shields
materials.description: Blackened metal with orange heat scorch, soot halos, heat tint, and blistered roughness.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate for furnace-room identity.

materials.use: Boiler shells, weapon sleeves, hot pipe brackets
materials.description: Oily black iron with blue-brown heat staining, scraped rims, and rivet-adjacent soot.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate, quieter than furnace metal and useful as a bridge material.

materials.use: Gauge faces, HUD inserts, pressure instruments
materials.description: Aged cream enamel dial with printed ticks, red needle motif, cracks, and dirty brass rim.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate. This is important for the pressure-pistol/HUD language.

materials.use: Secondary old gauge faces and background instrument clusters
materials.description: Darker old ivory enamel with fewer red accents, chipped rim dirt, and faded radial marks.
materials.blunt_note: Candidate. Useful filler, but less iconic than Gauge Face Enamel.

materials.use: Wall trim, door frames, floor thresholds, weapon bands
materials.description: Brass strip material with repeating rivets, edge polish, tarnish in recesses, and underside grime.
materials.blunt_note: Strong final candidate for north-star brass framing.

materials.use: Iron ribs, panel borders, grate supports
materials.description: Black iron trim with brass-touched rivet crowns, oily seams, and rubbed leading edges.
materials.blunt_note: Final candidate. Good for giving flat kit pieces a heavy industrial silhouette.

materials.use: Cold metal near vents, underside of pipes, damp machinery backs
materials.description: Dark metal with condensation trails, bead-like wet noise, and wiped streaks.
materials.blunt_note: Candidate. The wetness is useful, but can become noisy without careful scale.

materials.use: Temporary gasket, hose, and pressure seal material
materials.description: Black rubber gasket surface with cracks, dusted edges, and oil-dark wear.
materials.blunt_note: Placeholder. Included for completeness, but it does not move the corridor/gun visual target much.

runtime_contract: visual/material-only; no scenes, prefabs, meshes, gameplay scripts, colliders, audio, or build settings authored

v0_1_52_import_binding_assessment.pass_scope: 17 final-candidate materials are strong enough for quarantine import and selective corridor/weapon binding.
limitations: Overlay/decal candidates need a transparent/decal shader path before production placement.
Preview PNGs are procedural documentation boards, not Unity-editor scene captures.

dependencies: Unity Package Manager local package reference
known_risks: Preview PNGs are procedural documentation boards, not Unity-rendered scene captures.
Overlay/decal candidates need a transparent/decal shader path before production placement.
RMA masks preserve roughness/metallic/occlusion intent but current Standard shader binding still needs primary-project import validation.
rollback_path: delete isolated package root or remove local package reference com.brassworks.sidecar.surface-material-detail-set08
